section_title: INTRODUCTION TO ES MARKET REVIEW AND WEEKLY OUTLOOK
content:
Welcome to a short review, incorporating several concepts taught in the 2022 and 2023 mentorships. We
are analyzing a weekly chart of ES (E-mini S&P), specifically the June 2023 delivery contract.
Attention is drawn to an area where ES is likely to draw up, similar to how the NASDAQ futures contract
has already delivered its respective volume and bounce. This suggests technicals are pushing price higher.
It has been mentioned on Twitter this week that a run on the buy side is favored.

section_title: DISCOUNT FAIR VALUE GAP AND MARKET DRIVERS
content:
A Discount Fair Value Gap (FVG) is present between the high of one candle and the low of another. This
particular area was delivered twice.
The market is consolidating with a weekly imbalance, seen as a volume and bounce. If the buy side is
extrapolated, significant movement is expected. Fed Chair Powell is scheduled to speak on Friday at 10
AM, which is believed to be the reason for the market's current cautious posture, waiting for his remarks.
His speech will likely be used as a 'smoke screen' to move price.

section_title: WEEKLY PRICE OBJECTIVES AND CANDLE WICK INTERPRETATION
content:
On the weekly chart, analyze a down-closed candle. The opening price is used, not the candle's high,
because of a 'short, stubby little candle wick' relative to the entirety of its range. In such cases, the opening
price is elected for analysis.
Expect this weekly candle to expand, potentially reaching a specific high and then into the weekly volume
of balance. Thursday and Friday's action is anticipated to deliver this movement, mirroring prior NASDAQ
behavior.

section_title: TRANSPOSING WEEKLY LEVELS TO DAILY CHARTS
content:
Observe the blue shaded area, which represents an order block. These significant levels will transpose
directly onto the daily charts without confusion. The weekly order block level and the shaded weekly
discount FVG, previously mentioned as two candles trading as wicks on the weekly chart, are now visible
and shown through the daily candles.

section_title: DAILY IMBALANCES, LIQUIDITY DRAWS, AND KEY LEVELS
content:
Earlier this week on Twitter, a video vignette highlighted an imbalance a Fair Value Gap (FVG) between
one candle's high and another's low. This section of price action is a Buy Side Imbalance, Sell Side
Inefficiency (BISI).
The market has largely held within this imbalance, reaching up into a Premium Fair Value Gap (FVG).
Relative equal highs in the past also reside within the weekly volume imbalance. The weighted shaded
area, specifically 4244 (even), is identified as a draw on liquidity. While it may not be reached this week,
price is expected to reach the current high before the week closes. The determination of whether it stays
higher is yet to be seen, but it is currently treated as a draw on liquidity.
The purplish-blue and pink levels are crucial and will be visible on lower timeframes. These include the buy
side liquidity pool, the daily premium FVG, the daily discount FVG, and the weekly discount FVG. This
outlines the market's ebb and flow and the sustained focus on buy side liquidity.

section_title: BUY SIDE LIQUIDITY AND OFFSET DISTRIBUTION
content:
The buy side was outlined across various highs. A previous tweet noted a lack of runs on liquidity,
indicating that liquidity is being stacked up. This means that every time the market moved lower, it was
preparing to utilize this stacked liquidity as a means of offset distribution. This process offsets the longs
that were accumulated at various lower price points.

section_title: DAILY FAIR VALUE GAP GRADIENT LEVELS AND MISCONCEPTIONS
content:
Observe the dashed and dotted lines. These represent the gradient levels of the Fair Value Gap (FVG) on
the daily chart:
- The midpoint is the dotted red line, referred to as the Consequent Encroachment midpoint.
- The lower 25 percent.
- The upper 25 percent.
- The high and the low of that daily Fair Value Gap.
These levels originate from a specific area of price action. If you apply this to your own charts, you will
observe how price respects these levels.
It is crucial to understand that this is not 'Quarters Theory'. Rectangles are merely used to highlight areas
for study, not as a reinvention of supply and demand, which is not the trading methodology employed.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC PRECISION AND FUTURE PRICE EXPECTATIONS
content:
While not 'Quarters Theory,' the algorithm precisely seeks these levels within inefficiencies, particularly in a
Fair Value Gap. Observe how the market bodies respect these levels.
Today, the market rallied up into the premium Fair Value Gap on the daily chart and is consolidating. A
slight retracement lower is anticipated before price re-prices into the 4200s. This target represents
significant movement, especially with Fed Chair Powell speaking on Friday, as his words are often used to
'perpetuate movement'.

section_title: SHORT-TERM OUTLOOK AND EFFICIENT PRICE DELIVERY
content:
For tomorrow (Thursday) and Friday, ahead of Fed Chair Powell's discussion, attention is on a specific Fair
Value Gap (FVG) the low of one candle to the high of another. This FVG is a Sell Side Imbalance, Buy
Side Inefficiency (SIBI).
The market has currently only offered downside delivery. To efficiently 'rebook and balance' this area, the
market needs to reprice it, which implies an upside delivery. Efficient price delivery involves a 'pass above
and below or below and above' an 'up, down, up, down' movement to reprice an inefficiency.

section_title: LOWER TIMEFRAME ANALYSIS AND FVG GRADIENT LEVELS SETUP
content:
Reviewing a Buy Side Imbalance, Sell Side Inefficiency (BISI) and another gap, it is important to note
these, even if not kept on the charts going forward.
Dropping to a 15-minute timeframe, observe how the market respects the gradient levels on the Fair Value
Gap. To replicate this:
- Use a Fib tool and plot it on the high and low of any Fair Value Gap or gap.
- In Fib settings, highlight the 50 percent, 25 percent, and 75 percent levels.
- When drawing a range from high to low or low to high, these levels will appear. More detail on this will be
taught in coming months, but this is sufficient for the current method.

section_title: ICT BACKTESTING METHODOLOGY AND CHART JOURNALING
content:
The market's drop at the opening bell will be refined to a level of precision. This presentation demonstrates
the author's backtesting method, which differs from using Forex testers or market replays (those are for
forward testing before live data).
Effective backtesting involves:
- Going back into old data.
- Studying how price moves.
- Annotating charts in detail.
- Journaling, often using individual slides in a presentation format.
Students are encouraged to find a comfortable journaling method, as many options exist. The key is to be
highly detailed, making it like a 'therapy' or 'hobby' to thoroughly study historical price delivery and timings.
This extensive backtesting over months and years allows the subconscious to retain price action patterns
as 'pseudo experience'.

section_title: PSYCHOLOGICAL BENEFITS OF DETAILED BACKTESTING AND POSITIVE
content:
REINFORCEMENT
Annotate charts, particularly in empty spaces, with positive self-reinforcement, like 'cheerleading'
comments. For example, noting that you saw certain market behaviors before they happened. By retaining
this and reviewing annotations, the subconscious processes it as real experience.
This is a positive reinforcement loop:
- Never add negative remarks or complaints about missed expectations or incorrect predictions.
- Always cheerlead your insights. This trains the mind to reduce fear and recognize historical patterns. The
procedure builds confidence and pattern recognition from past observations.
5
- MINUTE CHART:

section_title: CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT AND PRECISION
content:
On the 5-minute chart, observe how the market bodies respect the Consequent Encroachment, which is
the midpoint of the high and low of the daily discount Fair Value Gap (FVG). This concept might be
advanced for newer students.
However, consistent practice with personal charts will reveal repeating patterns and the market's precision
in respecting these levels.

section_title: TIMEFRAME REFINEMENT AND THE ICT SILVER BULLET SETUP
content:
Students are taught to refine timeframes: from a 15-minute chart, down to a 5-minute, then a 3-minute, and
finally a 1-minute, until imbalances or the specific trading model (e.g., the Silver Bullet) are identified.
The ICT Silver Bullet setup was introduced in a Twitter Space (audio format) about 1.5-2 months prior. A
dedicated video presentation was made just before this review.
Today's morning session Silver Bullet is the primary focus, but a PM (post-meridiem) session Silver Bullet
is also visible: a shift in market structure, retrace into an FVG, and drop to attack sell side liquidity. This can
be used for personal study.

section_title: ES BULLISH BIAS AND JUDAS SWING ANALYSIS
content:
A bullish bias for ES was maintained, expecting it to catch up with NASDAQ. A tweet with a chart was sent
out highlighting buy side liquidity pools that the market was expected to engage.
At 9:30 AM, the market dropped in a 'Judas Swing', clearing out a short-term low. This move will be
examined in detail.

section_title: MORNING SILVER BULLET LONG SETUP
content:
Analyze the move in detail: A shift in market structure occurred at a short-term swing high, followed by a
Fair Value Gap (FVG) between 10:00 AM and 11:00 AM. Given the bullish bias, this drop into an FVG
represents a Silver Bullet Long setup.
- Entry: Buying within the FVG, using the candle's low as reference.
- Target: 10 handles (e.g., 4140 to 4130 baseline, aiming for above 4144).
- Profit-taking: Advised to take initial profits at 5 handles.
The setup offered the expected range. Observe that after hitting the buy side, price often drops back down
to take out the sell side.

section_title: SMART MONEY ACCUMULATION AND FVG RETRACEMENT
content:
The market's action of running stops serves a purpose: smart money accumulates new long positions while
retail traders' trailed stop losses are taken out. Subsequently, price trades back down into the daily discount
Fair Value Gap (FVG), specifically within its upper quadrant (the 75 percent level of that range).

section_title: UNDERSTANDING BALANCED PRICE RANGES AND INEFFICIENCIES
content:
It's critical to understand that when price trades up into an imbalance (which some might call a 'liquidity
void'), it is not necessarily 'balanced.' It has only 'rebooked and repriced to inefficiency' that is all it has
done; it has not become balanced yet.
A 'balanced price range' occurs when the market trades down to the low of that same imbalance again,
fails to go lower, respects that level, and then leaves the range. In this context, it has acted as a trading
range inside one micro imbalance on a three-minute chart.

section_title: PRECISION OF PRICE DELIVERY AND ONE-MINUTE CHART ANALYSIS
content:
Observe the precise relationship: this candle's high is exactly the low of another candle not one tick short
or off. This precision is verifiable by reviewing your own price data.
When such precision is seen, and price begins to move higher, it can be trusted as the likely low of the day.
Transitioning to a one-minute chart, we see this balanced price range between 10:00 AM and 11:00 AM
New York local time.

section_title: TRADINGVIEW SETTINGS AND OPENING RANGE GAP (ORG)
content:
On TradingView, during real-time price data viewing, electronic trading hours are typically highlighted. For
more detail:
- If you have paid for a live data feed on TradingView, you can toggle between 'Electronic Trading Hours'
and 'Regular Trading Hours' (RTH).
- Clicking this option allows you to switch to 'Regular Trading Hours'.
When toggled to RTH, your chart will reveal the 'Opening Range Gap' (ORG). This concept has been
taught since 1996 to charter members and old ICT mentorship students, predating recent discussions by
others.

section_title: INTERPRETING AND TRADING THE OPENING RANGE GAP (ORG)
content:
The Opening Range Gap (ORG) is defined by the closing price of the previous session and the 9:30 AM
opening price. How to use this information:
- Bullish Gap Higher: If a gap higher occurs when bullish, expect price to trade down to the gap, reprice it,
and then move higher, away from the direction of the gap, after it fills.
- Bearish Gap Lower: If bearish, expect a trade lower, fill the gap, a potential false run, and then a break
lower.
- Consolidation/Higher Timeframe Objective Met: If in consolidation or a higher timeframe weekly objective
has been met, a gap higher may fill, but then simply use the entire gap as a range throughout the day.

section_title: ORG IN BULLISH CONTEXT AND INEFFICIENCY CREATION
content:
In a bullish context, as highlighted previously, a gap higher at the opening fills and reprices down into the
'opening range gap low' (the previous day's Regular Trading Hours (RTH) close). This price can always be
found by toggling electronic trading hours to RTH.
This separation (hidden electronic trading from yesterday's settlement to the 9:30 AM New York opening
bell) creates a gap. Gaps, whether higher, lower, or unchanged, are desirable because they create
inefficiency. Inefficiencies are often, though not always, repriced to, and understanding this information is
paramount.

section_title: OPENING RANGE GAP (ORG) MULTIPLICATIONS AND PROJECTIONS
content:
A unique ICT concept involves the Opening Range Gap (ORG): once it is completely repriced in a bullish
market, you can multiply its range to project higher targets.
- Take the range from the low to the high of the gap.
- Multiply it up to get two standard deviations. This often brings price very close to the high, sometimes
without needing the premium Fair Value Gap (FVG) in the pink area.
Refer to previous Twitter posts and short video vignettes for more details on imbalances and how
everything trades.

section_title: POWER OF THREE AND DAILY RANGE ANALYSIS
content:
Applying the 'Power of Three' concept, the daily range for today unfolded as follows:
- The opening at 9:30 AM.
- A 'Judas Swing' where price trades down. Retail traders often chase this move lower, unaware of the
inefficiency between Regular Trading Hours (RTH) and how Electronic Trading Hours (ETH) are hidden.
There was trading, but the 9:30 AM opening bell brings initial volatility.
- The market seeks to reprice to the previous session's close, forming the low of the day.
- Price then begins to trade higher. Once it meaningfully moves above the opening price, it typically runs
higher for a premium specifically, the premium Fair Value Gap (FVG) shaded in pink. This analysis moves
from higher timeframes down, keeping track of salient points.

section_title: CONFLUENCES CONFIRMING THE LOW OF THE DAY
content:
The Power of Three concept illustrates the day's action: Open, Manipulation (Judas Swing), Accumulation
of Longs, Distribution into a Premium, and Day Close. The daily range was a classic ICT buy day, with
multiple confluences confirming the low:
- Judas Swing to reprice the Opening Range Gap low.
- Daily Fair Value Gap (FVG) consequent encroachment (midpoint) confluence.
- New Day Opening Gap (refer to a previous YouTube video for more detail).
These factors explain why price dropped to this specific low, a combination of confluences that retail logic
would miss. The high of the day was booked at the daily premium FVG high. All these details create a
tapestry of how the algorithm prices, reprices, redelivers, balances, and seeks new liquidity. This is the
essence of trading with these concepts: understanding if the algorithm will move higher or lower, why it
seeks inefficiency or takes out stops, or why it consolidates to frustrate traders.

section_title: INEFFICIENCY OR LIQUIDITY
content:
When trading with these concepts, you are essentially seeking to understand what the algorithm is likely to
do:
- Why should it move higher? For an inefficiency, or to take out buy stops.
- Why should it move lower? To trade to an inefficiency, or to take out sell stops.
- If it's not doing either of these, it will consolidate in a range-bound manner, frustrating traders. This
accurately describes market behavior seen over recent days and weeks.

section_title: DETAILED BACKTESTING AS A SELF-AUTHORED TRADING MANUAL
content:
A detailed backtesting procedure involves:
- Filling in observations in various areas on your charts.
- 'Cheerleading' yourself in annotations, noting how the market respected setups like the Silver Bullet (a
Fair Value Gap on the three-minute chart, transposed to the one-minute chart).
- Including every detail observed.
This deep journaling is a meditative process. It requires significant time, work, and effort to organize charts
so they are meaningful. You are essentially writing the most important and useful trading manual you will
ever read, using your own eyes, experience, annotations, charting, and snapshots across different
timeframes. This intense work over months leads to a superior ability to read price action; it's the 'secret
part of the recipe'.

section_title: HINDSIGHT STUDY AND BIAS
content:
All profitable students engage in this type of detailed study of old price moves:
- They examine every detail, including the exact timing of repricing to highs or lows.
- They establish a higher timeframe bias, understanding 'why' price should move to certain levels.
- With this mindset, they review charts to identify all opportunities that presented themselves.
Over time, this builds a rich 'tapestry' of hindsight price action reading, directly applicable to watching live
price action. This method mirrors professional training: doctors, technicians, and surgeons all practice with
hindsight case studies or cadavers, where there is 'no chance of harm.' In the same way, studying hindsight
price action with annotations is all 'upside' with no risk.

section_title: MAXIMIZING HINDSIGHT STUDY AND BROAD APPLICABILITY
content:
The only downside to this backtesting method is not doing it, or recording negative affirmations in your
annotations (e.g., 'I was stupid,' 'I missed this,' 'this doesn't work'). Instead, always 'cheerlead' yourself so
your journal entries, in your own words, reinforce positive observations.
Map out each individual day in detail. This is why it's advised to focus on one market (or two if closely
correlated) to dedicate maximum time and energy. The principles demonstrated (Forex, Commodities,
Futures) are applicable to every asset. With that, be safe.
